title: polygala vulgaris proles involutiflora
intro: Polygala vulgaris proles involutiflora, also known as the common polygala or running milkwort, is a perennial plant species within the milkwort family (Polygalaceae). It is a flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, with medicinal properties attributed to its roots and leaves, which are used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting kidney function. The herbal preparations of Polygala vulgaris proles involutiflora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
